markers.evidence_type: myelin basic protein, defining marker of myelinating Schwann cells
myelin protein zero (P0), the most abundant myelin protein in PNS
pan-Schwann cell marker (expressed in both myelinating and non-myelinating subtypes)

other_findings.finding: Myelinating Schwann cells (Schwann_1) are defined by high expression of MBP, MPZ, and PLP1, which together form the compact myelin sheath around large-diameter peripheral nerve axons
Two Schwann cell subtypes exist in adult human skin: myelinating (MPZ+/MBP+) and non-myelinating (NGFR+/GFAP+/GLUL+)

Myelinating Schwann cells in skin ensheath large myelinated nerve fibres (A-beta fibres) involved in mechanoreception, while non-myelinating Schwann cells form Remak bundles around unmyelinated C-fibres

evidence_quotes.quote: Myelinating Schwann cells are characterised by high expression of MPZ, MBP and PLP1, which encode the major structural proteins of compact peripheral myelin
evidence_quotes.source_file: Zhang et al. (2025) Single-cell atlas of peripheral nervous system cells in human skin

evidence_quotes.quote: MPZ (P0) is the most abundant protein in peripheral nervous system (PNS) myelin, comprising over 50% of total PNS myelin protein, and is exclusively expressed in myelinating Schwann cells
evidence_quotes.source_file: Ioghen et al. (2020) Molecular aspects of peripheral myelination
evidence_quotes.context: MPZ as defining myelinating Schwann cell marker
